patch.value:
## Summary

pm-i84i was closed 2026-06-20T01:37 after replacing `file://`-URL TypeScript source imports (`tests/helpers/sourceModule.ts`) with Vitest-transformable relative specifiers and adding a per-PR `windows-regression` job. But the next nightly (run 27864828585, 2026-06-20T07:52, commit 88aa1cbf — which already contained that fix) was **still red**.

Root cause: the **other** test helper, `tests/helpers/scriptModule.ts` (`createScriptHarness`), was not touched. Its `importModule`/`importModuleStable` import repo `.mjs` scripts via an absolute `pathToFileURL(...).href` (+ cache-bust query). On Windows, Vitest does **not** intercept that `file:///D:/...` specifier for transformation, so the script's leading `#!/usr/bin/env node` shebang survives into the module compiler and throws `SyntaxError: Invalid or unexpected token` at the `#`. Every test in the 28 `tests/unit/scripts/**` specs (plus the OTEL integration spec that imports a script) fails identically — matching the observed fan-out (dogfood-package-first 31/31, finalize-build 3/3, bundle-cli, run-tests, release/*, etc.).

The per-PR `windows-regression` job pm-i84i added only ran `cli-main-errors.spec.ts` + `runtime-schema-path-win32-guard.spec.ts`, so it never exercised the script-harness path — which is why the per-PR check stayed green while the nightly went red again.

## Fix

1. `tests/helpers/scriptModule.ts`: route both `importModule` and `importModuleStable` through a relative specifier computed from the helper's own module URL (`../../<relativePath>`, POSIX separators), mirroring `sourceModule.ts`. Vitest then transforms the module and strips the shebang on every platform.
2. Broaden the per-PR `windows-regression` CI job to also run the whole `tests/unit/scripts/` directory, so this entire class fails a required per-PR check, not just the nightly. Lock it in `ci-workflow-contract.spec.ts`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `scriptModule.ts` imports scripts via relative specifiers; no absolute `file://` URL.
- [ ] All `tests/unit/scripts/**` specs pass on Linux (no regression) and on windows-latest in the per-PR job.
- [ ] Per-PR `windows-regression` job covers `tests/unit/scripts/`.
- [ ] Coverage stays 100/100/100/100; no new `any`.

## Evidence

- Still-red nightly: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/actions/runs/27864828585 (commit 88aa1cbf, post-pm-i84i)
- GitHub issue: #315
Nightly windows-latest STILL red after pm-i84i: scriptModule test harness imports .mjs scripts via absolute file:// URL, shebang survives missing transform (SyntaxError)

Nightly windows-latest / Node 20 remained red after the pm-i84i fix (run 27864828585, 2026-06-20T07:52, on commit 88aa1cbf which already contained the pm-i84i remediation). pm-i84i only fixed the sourceModule.ts TypeScript-source-import path; the scriptModule.ts harness (28 script specs) was missed and fails with the same SyntaxError class.

value.note: Route .mjs script imports through Windows-safe relative specifiers
Broaden per-PR windows-regression job to cover tests/unit/scripts/

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text:
Root-cause + fix iteration (verified against the real per-PR windows-latest job, not just nightly):

1. The differentiator is NOT absolute-vs-relative specifier — it is whether Vite TRANSFORMS the module. A fully dynamic `import()` (specifier begins with `${...}`) is opaque to Vite, so on Windows the `.mjs` is loaded untransformed and its `#!/usr/bin/env node` shebang reaches the module compiler → `SyntaxError: Invalid or unexpected token` (empty stack). On Linux the runtime resolver happens to transform it, which is why it was green there.

2. First attempt (relative `file://`-free specifier computed from the helper dir) STILL failed the windows-regression job — still fully dynamic, still untransformed.

3. Working fix: Vite's `dynamic-import-vars` plugin only rewrites an `import()` when the static parts include a directory prefix AND a file extension, and the variable may span only ONE path segment. So `../../scripts/${name}.mjs` works; `../../${path}` (no static ext) is rejected ('file extension must be in the static part'), and `../../scripts/${'release/utils'}.mjs` is rejected ('variables only represent file names one level deep') — hence the explicit `scripts/release/${name}.mjs` branch for the single nested dir.

4. The per-call cache-bust query had to be dropped (it broke the glob-map lookup: 'Unknown variable dynamic import: …?v=n1'); `vi.resetModules()` in the harness afterEach already provides per-test freshness and keeps relative `vi.doMock` targets matching.

5. Hardened the per-PR `windows-regression` CI job to run all of `tests/unit/scripts/` so this class is caught per-PR (it is what surfaced both the original miss and my first failed attempt within minutes instead of waiting for nightly).
